title: Evangelion cameos
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion cameos?             Quote Post by Israfel777 » Fri Aug 11, 2006 1:10 am I don't know if this has been made already. But I thought I'd bring it up. I've seen a couple of Eva cameos in some games or such. Things like Evas being somewhere in another series, the use of the themes in an anime, and even odd versions of the pilots. Right now, there's only one I can mention right off hand, and that's from Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi. Episode 3 : Sci-Fi Abenobashi. They had one part that used the battle theme from Eva in it, and even attempted to interpret an Angel into the episode. Any others?

[]:             Quote Post by SSGokuJr » Fri Aug 11, 2006 6:44 am The D Type Equipment was in FFVII at the Gold Saucer, and I remeber somwhere there was a mech that looke like Eva 1 In a Justice Leauge Episode.

[]: Re: Evangelion cameos?             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Fri Aug 11, 2006 9:10 am Israfel777 wrote: I don't know if this has been made already. But I thought I'd bring it up. I've seen a couple of Eva cameos in some games or such. Things like Evas being somewhere in another series, the use of the themes in an anime, and even odd versions of the pilots. Right now, there's only one I can mention right off hand, and that's from Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi. Episode 3 : Sci-Fi Abenobashi. They had one part that used the battle theme from Eva in it, and even attempted to interpret an Angel into the episode. Any others?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Aug 11, 2006 2:17 pm In Excel Saga, episode 1, Rikdo's room is full of anime memorabilia. Among these is a (if I recall correctly) white robot thingy with pylons (sp?) on it's shoulders.

[]: Re: Evangelion cameos?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 11, 2006 2:46 pm Timesplitter 01 wrote: GITS: SAC 2nd (episode 20)- A Tachikoma comments that the entrance to the underground submarine base looks like a "Geofront". This is a reference to Neon Genesis Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Fri Aug 11, 2006 4:22 pm I'm pretty sure the last episode of that Haruhoorayhi Suzumammamia show had a choking reference to The End of Evangelion in it, but it isn't a cameo per say.

[]:             Quote Post by Ripple_in_Eternity » Fri Aug 11, 2006 5:30 pm [deleted by user]

[]: Re: Evangelion cameos?             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Fri Aug 11, 2006 8:49 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Timesplitter 01 wrote: GITS: SAC 2nd (episode 20)- A Tachikoma comments that the entrance to the underground submarine base looks like a "Geofront". This is a reference to Neon Genesis Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Aug 11, 2006 11:04 pm Not that it counts, but... I threw a couple of NGE references into a screenplay I've been working on. If it ever makes it to the big screen, keep an ear open for this bit: [code:1] SEIKO â€œReiko Ayanamiâ€... She lies back and puts her hands behind her head. SEIKO That was the name I was supposed to put on anything I had to fill out while I was there... Just a prodigy on loan from MacArthur base. KURZ Wasn't that the name of a cartoon character? SEIKO Yeah... from Freon Hypothesis Revelation or something like that. Before our time, probably. She rolls over. SEIKO Wake me up early, okay?[/code:1]

[]:             Quote Post by Rei_Unit00 » Sat Aug 12, 2006 1:06 am In episode 2, Fire Starter (I believe), of Fooly Cooly, Kamon, Naota's father goes on this rant because of Canti and then Haruko asks what that ment, then Naota said that Kamon "Wrote a book on the deep mysteries of EVA". I can't remember the rant, though. And in one episode of Kappa Mikey (I actually watch that. It's fun to spot the anime cameos, It's like "I Spy"), a random character that was seen once looks like Rei Ayanami.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Aug 12, 2006 7:10 am You can find a bunch more cameos on the original thread for this Here

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Aug 12, 2006 10:56 am If you want to be a bastard, you can say the entirety of RahXephon fits into this label. Anyway... Could be just coincidence, but Yuki from 'The Melancholy of Haruhi Suzumiya' reminds me of Rei. Also, Digimon Froniter's Wargrowlmon (sp?) reminds much of Sho-chan. D-Ripper reminds of LCL, but only faintly.

[]:             Quote Post by dogmai » Sun Aug 13, 2006 5:11 am in keroro gunso/sgt. frog when fuyuki and the girl are telling keroro about their mother there is a pic of unit01 in the background

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Sun Aug 13, 2006 2:03 pm Rei_Unit00 wrote: In episode 2, Fire Starter (I believe), of Fooly Cooly, Kamon, Naota's father goes on this rant because of Canti and then Haruko asks what that ment, then Naota said that Kamon "Wrote a book on the deep mysteries of EVA".

[]:             Quote Post by Kaworu Nagisa » Sun Aug 13, 2006 10:38 pm The end of Kingdom Hearts 2 was very End of Evaish (SPOILERS) - Crazy villain is willing to take evrybodies hearts away to make everybody a non-human. - Hearts fall down to the earth in a very "lilith soul reaper" esque sequence - Close to final scene has two characters by themselves on a surreal beach. - Kairi gives Sora a very "Yuish" line about hearts, and it ultimately opens up a door to the happy ending evrybody wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 14, 2006 1:33 am Ditto Keroro Gunso ep#48

[]:             Quote Post by darth_mark » Mon Aug 14, 2006 2:38 am the3rdchildren wrote: I'm pretty sure the last episode of that Haruhoorayhi Suzumammamia show had a choking reference to The End of Evangelion in it, but it isn't a cameo per say.

[]:             Quote Post by SSGokuJr » Mon Aug 14, 2006 3:23 am Kaworu Nagisa wrote: The end of Kingdom Hearts 2 was very End of Evaish (SPOILERS) - Crazy villain is willing to take evrybodies hearts away to make everybody a non-human. - Hearts fall down to the earth in a very "lilith soul reaper" esque sequence - Close to final scene has two characters by themselves on a surreal beach. - Kairi gives Sora a very "Yuish" line about hearts, and it ultimately opens up a door to the happy ending evrybody wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Israfel777 » Mon Aug 14, 2006 8:40 am Legend of the Past wrote: If you want to be a bastard, you can say the entirety of RahXephon fits into this label.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Mon Aug 14, 2006 9:21 am Israfel777 wrote: Legend of the Past wrote: If you want to be a bastard, you can say the entirety of RahXephon fits into this label.
